segments.text: Okay, one of the most common questions I get is, should you make a new account or start posting your new content on the account you're watching this on right
now, and after being asked this question a couple hundred times over the couple years I've been online, my answer is very simple. Post your new content on
your old account, unless you are going to be self-conscious of the people that are going to be watching on your old account, therefore holding you back from
actually posting the content. If you care about what other people think of your content, then go ahead, start a new account and start fresh. If that's going
to help you get the new content out there, then go for it. But the reason why I suggest for people to stay on the
same account is because you have years of personal connections. It's essentially your whole contacts list on one Instagram account. So, even if you
make a new Instagram account and do your collab post and tell people to go go over there and follow my new account,
nobody is going to. Some people are going to be off of Instagram. Some people are going to miss the post. Some people are just not going to care enough to follow you on two accounts.
Therefore, you're leaving so much opportunity, potential money, and a boost to your new account by starting
fresh, starting over. But here's the thing that a lot of people miss. A lot of people feel like the algorithm on their current account, on their old account, is tainted. It's already messed
up. It's already like not hitting or there's just going to be not enough engagement on their old account. But the reality is the people that don't want to
see it, the haters, the old friends, the people that only want to see the old stuff will unfollow you or they will stop using the app and then Instagram
will delete their account eventually. So after 3 to 6 months of posting your new style of content, everything will even out. everything will balance out. The
algorithm will know who your new audience is targeting, who your new content is targeting, and everything's going to sort itself out. And if you
started a new account, you wouldn't have that problem to begin with. So, it's still going to take you 3 to 6 months to gain momentum anyways. So, either
decision you go to, it's going to take you 3 to 6 months to really build up the momentum that you need to let the algorithm know what target audience you
want to be hitting. And then in one of the outcomes, you have years of personal connections and friends that will, you know, grow your account, word of mouth,
grow your brand, grow your product, grow your business, whatever you're trying to do. And the other, you don't get any of that and you have to start from absolutely zero. This account right now
has trash engagement. Right now, it's at 198,000 followers. It peaked at like 230,000 followers. I've lost 35,000
followers in over 6 months because right now I'm just at a low because I don't have that momentum of my account right now. But eventually once it picks back
up, I'll reach 300,000, 350, whatever the new high is going to be. I've never made a new account. I've gone viral in a whole bunch of different niches. I've
had so many different identities and changes, but I just let that be a public portfolio. I let whoever follow me follow me, and whoever wants to leave
can unfollow and leave. Hopefully that helps. Peace.
